title: Storage account 'acmedata01' allows non-HTTPS traffic
description: Storage account acmedata01 does not enforce HTTPS-only traffic. Non-HTTPS connections transmit data in plain text, exposing it to interception. Enable the "Secure transfer required" setting.

title: Storage account 'acmedata01' allows public blob access
description: Storage account acmedata01 has public blob access enabled at the account level. This allows individual containers to be configured for anonymous public access. Disable public blob access to prevent accidental data exposure.

title: Storage account 'acmedata01' does not use customer-managed keys
description: Storage account acmedata01 stores sensitive data but uses Microsoft-managed keys instead of customer-managed keys (CMK) from Key Vault. CMK provides additional control over encryption key lifecycle and rotation.

title: Storage account 'acmearchive01' does not use customer-managed keys
description: Storage account acmearchive01 stores sensitive data but uses Microsoft-managed keys instead of customer-managed keys (CMK) from Key Vault. CMK provides additional control over encryption key lifecycle and rotation.

title: Storage account 'acmearchive01' allows TLS version below 1.2
description: Storage account acmearchive01 is configured with minimum TLS version TLS1_1. TLS 1.0 and 1.1 have known vulnerabilities. Set minimum TLS version to TLS1_2 or higher.

title: Storage account 'acmeml01' does not use customer-managed keys
description: Storage account acmeml01 stores sensitive data but uses Microsoft-managed keys instead of customer-managed keys (CMK) from Key Vault. CMK provides additional control over encryption key lifecycle and rotation.

title: Key Vault 'kv-prod' has soft delete disabled
description: Key Vault kv-prod does not have soft delete enabled. Without soft delete, deleted keys, secrets, and certificates are permanently lost immediately. Enable soft delete to allow recovery of accidentally deleted items within the retention period.

title: Key Vault 'kv-prod' does not have purge protection enabled
description: Key Vault kv-prod does not have purge protection enabled. Without purge protection, a malicious actor with sufficient permissions can permanently delete vault items even during the soft-delete retention period. Enable purge protection to prevent irreversible deletion.

title: Key Vault 'kv-prod' uses access policies instead of RBAC
description: Key Vault kv-prod uses legacy access policies instead of Azure RBAC for authorization. RBAC provides finer-grained, auditable access control that integrates with Azure AD Privileged Identity Management (PIM). Migrate to RBAC authorization model.

title: SQL DB 'acme-prod' has Transparent Data Encryption disabled
description: SQL Database acme-prod on server sql-acme-prod does not have Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) enabled. TDE encrypts database files at rest. Enable TDE to protect data from unauthorized access to the underlying storage.

title: SQL server 'sql-acme-prod' does not have auditing enabled
description: SQL server sql-acme-prod does not have database auditing configured. Auditing tracks database events and provides visibility into activity that could indicate suspicious behavior. Enable server-level auditing and direct logs to a storage account or Log Analytics workspace.

title: SQL server 'sql-acme-prod' has Advanced Threat Protection disabled
description: SQL server sql-acme-prod does not have Advanced Threat Protection (ATP) enabled. ATP is a server-level setting that protects all databases on the server. Enable ATP for proactive threat detection across all databases.

title: SQL server 'sql-acme-prod' has public network access enabled
description: SQL server sql-acme-prod allows connections from public networks. This increases the attack surface. Disable public network access and use Private Link or VNet service endpoints to restrict access to trusted networks.

title: SQL server 'sql-acme-dev' does not have auditing enabled
description: SQL server sql-acme-dev does not have database auditing configured. Auditing tracks database events and provides visibility into activity that could indicate suspicious behavior. Enable server-level auditing and direct logs to a storage account or Log Analytics workspace.

title: SQL server 'sql-acme-dev' has Advanced Threat Protection disabled
description: SQL server sql-acme-dev does not have Advanced Threat Protection (ATP) enabled. ATP is a server-level setting that protects all databases on the server. Enable ATP for proactive threat detection across all databases.

title: SQL server 'sql-acme-dev' has public network access enabled
description: SQL server sql-acme-dev allows connections from public networks. This increases the attack surface. Disable public network access and use Private Link or VNet service endpoints to restrict access to trusted networks.

title: SQL DB 'acme-analytics' has Transparent Data Encryption disabled
description: SQL Database acme-analytics on server sql-acme-analytics does not have Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) enabled. TDE encrypts database files at rest. Enable TDE to protect data from unauthorized access to the underlying storage.

title: SQL server 'sql-acme-analytics' has public network access enabled
description: SQL server sql-acme-analytics allows connections from public networks. This increases the attack surface. Disable public network access and use Private Link or VNet service endpoints to restrict access to trusted networks.

title: AKS cluster 'aks-prod' has Kubernetes RBAC disabled
description: AKS cluster aks-prod does not have Kubernetes RBAC enabled. Without RBAC, all authenticated users have unrestricted access to the cluster API server. Enable RBAC to implement least-privilege access control for cluster resources.

title: AKS cluster 'aks-prod' does not have network policy enforcement enabled
description: AKS cluster aks-prod does not enforce Kubernetes NetworkPolicy. Without network policies, all pods can communicate with all other pods. Implement network policies to restrict pod-to-pod traffic to required communication paths only.

title: AKS cluster 'aks-prod' is not a private cluster
description: AKS cluster aks-prod exposes its API server to the public internet. A private cluster restricts API server access to the VNet, significantly reducing the attack surface. Migrate to a private cluster configuration.

title: AKS cluster 'aks-prod' lacks Azure AD integration
description: AKS cluster aks-prod is not integrated with Azure Active Directory for authentication. AAD integration enables managed identity-based auth and Azure RBAC for Kubernetes. Enable AAD integration to leverage enterprise identity and conditional access policies.

title: AKS cluster 'aks-dev' does not have network policy enforcement enabled
description: AKS cluster aks-dev does not enforce Kubernetes NetworkPolicy. Without network policies, all pods can communicate with all other pods. Implement network policies to restrict pod-to-pod traffic to required communication paths only.

title: VM 'vm-jumpbox-01' does not have disk encryption enabled
description: Virtual machine vm-jumpbox-01 does not have Azure Disk Encryption enabled. Disk encryption protects data at rest on OS and data disks using Azure Key Vault keys. Enable ADE to protect against unauthorized access if physical storage media is compromised.

title: VM 'vm-jumpbox-01' does not use a managed identity
description: Virtual machine vm-jumpbox-01 does not have a system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity. Managed identities eliminate the need for credentials in application code when accessing Azure services. Enable managed identity to improve credential hygiene.

title: VM 'vm-app-01' does not have disk encryption enabled
description: Virtual machine vm-app-01 does not have Azure Disk Encryption enabled. Disk encryption protects data at rest on OS and data disks using Azure Key Vault keys. Enable ADE to protect against unauthorized access if physical storage media is compromised.

title: VM 'vm-build-01' does not have disk encryption enabled
description: Virtual machine vm-build-01 does not have Azure Disk Encryption enabled. Disk encryption protects data at rest on OS and data disks using Azure Key Vault keys. Enable ADE to protect against unauthorized access if physical storage media is compromised.

title: NSG 'nsg-bastion' allows SSH (port 22) from 0.0.0.0/0
description: Network Security Group nsg-bastion has rule "Allow-SSH-Inbound" that allows inbound SSH access from 0.0.0.0/0. This exposes SSH to the entire internet. Restrict the source to specific IP ranges or use Azure Bastion.

title: NSG 'nsg-bastion' has no flow logs configured
description: Network Security Group nsg-bastion does not have NSG flow logs enabled. Flow logs are essential for monitoring network traffic, detecting anomalies, and meeting compliance requirements. Enable flow logs and send them to a storage account or Log Analytics workspace.

title: NSG 'nsg-app-tier' allows RDP (port 3389) from 0.0.0.0/0
description: Network Security Group nsg-app-tier has rule "Allow-RDP-Inbound" that allows inbound RDP access from Internet. This exposes RDP to the entire internet. Restrict the source to specific IP ranges or use Azure Bastion.

title: NSG 'nsg-app-tier' has no flow logs configured
description: Network Security Group nsg-app-tier does not have NSG flow logs enabled. Flow logs are essential for monitoring network traffic, detecting anomalies, and meeting compliance requirements. Enable flow logs and send them to a storage account or Log Analytics workspace.

title: NSG 'nsg-data-tier' has no flow logs configured
description: Network Security Group nsg-data-tier does not have NSG flow logs enabled. Flow logs are essential for monitoring network traffic, detecting anomalies, and meeting compliance requirements. Enable flow logs and send them to a storage account or Log Analytics workspace.

title: NSG 'nsg-devops' allows SSH (port 22) from 0.0.0.0/0
description: Network Security Group nsg-devops has rule "Allow-SSH-Inbound" that allows inbound SSH access from Internet. This exposes SSH to the entire internet. Restrict the source to specific IP ranges or use Azure Bastion.

title: RBAC: 5 users have Owner role at subscription scope
description: The subscription has 5 principals assigned the Owner role. The Owner role grants full access including the ability to assign roles. Limit Owner assignments to reduce risk of privilege abuse. Best practice recommends no more than 3 Owner assignments.

title: Custom role 'CustomRole-LegacyAdmin' has wildcard (*) actions
description: Custom role definition "CustomRole-LegacyAdmin" assigned to "sp-legacy-automation" grants wildcard (*) actions, equivalent to Owner-level access. Scope down the custom role to only required actions.

title: Subscription has 1 classic administrator(s)
description: The subscription still has 1 classic administrator(s) (Co-Administrator/Service Administrator). Classic admin roles are a legacy access model that bypasses Azure RBAC. Migrate to Azure RBAC roles and remove classic administrator assignments.

title: 2 guest user(s) have role assignments in the subscription
description: Found 2 guest (external) user(s) with Azure RBAC role assignments: vendor@external.com, auditor@thirdparty.com. Guest users represent external identities that may not be subject to your organization's security policies. Review and remove unnecessary guest access.

title: Azure Activity Log is not exported to a Log Analytics workspace
description: The Azure Activity Log is not being forwarded to a Log Analytics workspace or storage account. The Activity Log records subscription-level events including administrative actions, service health incidents, and policy writes. Export it for centralized SIEM integration.

title: No Log Analytics workspace configured for centralized log aggregation
description: There is no Log Analytics workspace connected to the subscription for centralized log collection. Without a centralized workspace, security events across resources are not aggregated, making threat detection and incident response significantly harder.

title: Key Vault diagnostic settings not configured for audit logging
description: Key Vault resources in the subscription do not have diagnostic settings configured to capture audit logs. Key Vault audit logs record all access to secrets, keys, and certificates. Enable diagnostic settings to send Key Vault logs to Log Analytics for security monitoring.

title: Microsoft Defender for Servers is not enabled (Free tier)
description: Microsoft Defender for Servers is not enabled. Without it, virtual machines lack vulnerability assessment, endpoint protection monitoring, and threat detection capabilities. Upgrade to the Standard tier to enable Defender for Servers.

title: Microsoft Defender for SQL is not enabled
description: Microsoft Defender for SQL is not enabled. Defender for SQL provides Advanced Threat Protection (ATP) and vulnerability assessments for Azure SQL Databases and SQL Servers on machines. Enable it to detect anomalous database activity.

title: Microsoft Defender for Storage is not enabled
description: Microsoft Defender for Storage is not enabled. Defender for Storage detects unusual and potentially harmful attempts to access or exploit storage accounts, including malware uploads and sensitive data exposure. Enable it to protect storage resources.

title: Microsoft Defender for Containers is not enabled
description: Microsoft Defender for Containers is not enabled. Defender for Containers provides runtime threat protection for AKS clusters, registry vulnerability scanning, and Kubernetes security posture management. Enable it to secure containerized workloads.
